_note: Blueprint for a standalone World Cup guide page. This is intentionally not tied to individual fixtures so it stays useful without live data.

page.title: World Cup Primer
page.subtitle: A standalone fan guide that explains the sport, the tournament, and the personalities around it without pretending to be a live command center.

fanModes.label: Brand New
fanModes.promise: Get the sport in plain English fast.

fanModes.label: Casual Fan
fanModes.promise: Learn enough to sound sharp while watching.

fanModes.label: I Know Ball
fanModes.promise: Skip straight to shape, style, and personality.

search.placeholder: Search offside, formations, missing stars, teams, fun facts...

sections.label: Start Here
sections.description: The fastest possible on-ramp for someone who just wants to follow a match without feeling lost.

sections.label: How Soccer Is Built
sections.description: Explains club vs country and where the World Cup sits in the larger ecosystem.

sections.label: Positions and Roles
sections.description: A quick decoder for who does what on the field.

sections.label: Formations Made Simple
sections.description: Tells users what the famous number patterns actually mean in human language.

sections.label: Watch Smart
sections.description: Turns casual viewers into active viewers by giving them a few smart cues to look for.

sections.label: World Cup Primer
sections.description: Why this tournament feels bigger than normal football and how the 2026 format works.

sections.label: Famous Missing Names
sections.description: Answers the question casual fans ask constantly: why is that player not here?

sections.label: Adopt A Team
sections.description: Helps neutral fans pick a side based on play style, atmosphere, and emotional chaos.

sections.label: Fun Facts
sections.description: The snackable section for records, weirdness, and lore.

interfaceNotes: Do not present timestamps in a way that implies live updates unless a real refresh pipeline exists.
Show source chips or info drawers on facts, rules, and absence cards so trust stays high.
Let search work across titles, player names, teams, and tags from every referenced collection.
Keep the first screen skimmable. This page should feel like an inviting guide, not a textbook.
